name: Heart Attack
description: Possible heart attack needs immediate emergency response.
dos: Help person sit and stay calm
donts: Do not leave person alone
Do not give heavy food or drinks
warning: LIFE THREATENING - Emergency services required immediately

name: Drowning
description: Water inhalation can quickly reduce oxygen supply.
dos: Move person to a safe dry place
Start CPR if trained and no breathing
donts: Do not delay emergency call
Do not shake the person violently
warning: LIFE THREATENING - Emergency services required immediately

name: Vomiting
dos: Give small sips of oral rehydration solution
donts: Do not force solid food immediately
Do not give very sugary drinks

name: Dehydration
description: Loss of fluids causes weakness and dizziness.
donts: Do not wait if confusion appears
Do not give only caffeinated drinks

subtypes.label: First Degree
dos: Cool with running water for at least 10 minutes
donts: Do not apply ice
Do not apply butter or toothpaste

subtypes.label: Second Degree
subtypes.description: Blisters and deeper skin damage with severe pain.
donts: Do not pop blisters
Do not apply oils or powders

subtypes.label: Third Degree
subtypes.description: Charred or white skin, possible numbness due to nerve damage.
donts: Do not pour water on severe burn
Do not remove stuck clothing
subtypes.warning: LIFE THREATENING - Emergency services required immediately

name: Snake Bite
description: Venomous bites can rapidly become life threatening.
donts: Do not cut or suck wound
Do not apply tight tourniquet
warning: LIFE THREATENING - Emergency services required immediately

name: Frostbite
description: Cold injury causes numb, pale, or hard skin.
dos: Warm area gradually with body heat or lukewarm water
donts: Do not rub affected skin
Do not use direct high heat

name: Sprain
description: Ligament injury causing swelling and pain.
donts: Do not force movement
Do not resume activity too early

name: Muscle Cramp
donts: Do not overstretch abruptly
Do not continue strenuous activity immediately

name: Bleeding / Cuts & Bruises
description: External injuries need cleaning, pressure, and observation.
dos: Apply direct pressure with clean cloth
Clean minor wounds with clean water
donts: Do not remove deeply embedded objects
Do not apply dirty materials

subtypes.label: Greenstick
subtypes.description: Partial bone crack, common in children.
donts: Do not force straightening
Do not move repeatedly

subtypes.label: Spiral
donts: Do not rotate limb
Do not walk on injured limb

subtypes.label: Comminuted
donts: Do not apply forceful pressure
Do not move patient unnecessarily
subtypes.warning: Likely severe injury - urgent care required

subtypes.label: Compound
subtypes.description: Open fracture with bone protrusion and bleeding risk.
donts: Do not push exposed bone back
Do not wash deep open fracture aggressively
subtypes.warning: LIFE THREATENING - Emergency services required immediately

subtypes.label: Dislocation
subtypes.description: Bone displaced from normal joint position.
donts: Do not force joint back
Do not move joint repeatedly
